description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, dark metallic appearance. The axe features two large, symmetrical crescent-shaped blades with a pointed tip at the top, giving it a menacing and powerful look. The handle is cylindrical, predominantly black, and adorned with metallic bands and subtle decorative accents, possibly brass or bronze, near the grip and midsection. The grip area appears textured for better handling, and the pommel is capped with a pointed metal end. The overall design is both functional and ornamental, suggesting it could serve as a ceremonial or display piece as well as a tool or weapon. The finish is matte to semi-glossy, with the dark coloration dominating the visual impression.
description: A double-headed, dark metallic battle axe with crescent blades and a decorated, textured handle. The design is both functional and ornamental, with a robust and menacing appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large crescent-shaped blades and the full decorated handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the pointed top and pommel.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the symmetrical double blades and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and pointed ends.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood or metal core, with dark finish, metallic bands, and a pointed pommel; textured grip for secure handling.
attribute_blade_design: Double crescent-shaped blades with symmetrical edges, pointed top, no beard, and subtle decorative elements; robust and wide cutting surfaces.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or pinned mounting, with visible integration of blade and handle.
